exclusion_rules.description: Only children aged 0 to 18 years are eligible for screening and treatment under RBSK

family_criteria.family_definition: Universal child health programme — every child aged 0-18 is eligible regardless of family income, caste, or location. Screening conducted at schools and anganwadi centres.

covered_procedures: Screening for Birth Defects: Neural tube defects, Down syndrome, cleft lip/palate, club foot, congenital heart disease, developmental dysplasia of hip, congenital cataract, hearing impairment
Screening for Childhood Diseases: Rheumatic heart disease, reactive airway disease, dental caries, otitis media, skin conditions, convulsive disorders
Screening for Deficiencies: Anaemia, vitamin A/D deficiency, severe acute malnutrition, goitre/iodine deficiency, rickets
Screening for Development Delays: Motor delay, cognitive delay, language delay, behavioural disorders (autism, ADHD, learning disability)
Free treatment at District Early Intervention Centre (DEIC)
Free referral to tertiary hospitals for surgical conditions
Free corrective surgery for birth defects (cleft lip, club foot, congenital heart defects)
Free spectacles, hearing aids, and prosthetic devices
excluded_procedures: Treatment of adults or children above 18 years
Treatment at private hospitals (unless referred by DEIC)
Conditions not covered under the 4D framework
Cosmetic procedures unrelated to birth defects

required_documents.name: School/Anganwadi Enrollment
required_documents.notes: Screening happens at the institution. No document needed for screening. For treatment referral, age proof may be needed.

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card (Child/Parent)
required_documents.notes: For DEIC registration and referral tracking. Not required for initial screening.

required_documents.name: RBSK Screening Card
required_documents.notes: Issued by mobile health team after screening. Required for referral to DEIC.

enrollment_channels: Government and government-aided schools (for children 6-18 years)
Primary Health Centre (walk-in for out-of-school children)
enrollment_steps: Step 1: No enrollment needed — RBSK mobile health teams visit anganwadis and schools on schedule
Step 2: Dedicated RBSK mobile teams (2 per block: 1 male, 1 female AYUSH doctor) screen children
Step 3: Children screened for 30+ conditions across 4Ds (Defects, Diseases, Deficiencies, Delays)
Step 4: Children with identified conditions referred to District Early Intervention Centre (DEIC)
Step 5: DEIC provides free confirmatory diagnosis and treatment planning
Step 6: Complex cases referred to tertiary hospitals empanelled under RBSK for free surgery/treatment
Step 7: Follow-up tracked through RBSK portal and mobile health team visits
description_for_embedding: Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK) is a central government universal child health screening and treatment programme under the National Health Mission. It screens all children aged 0-18 years for 30+ conditions across 4Ds: Birth Defects, Childhood Diseases, Nutritional Deficiencies, and Development Delays — through mobile health teams visiting anganwadis and schools. Treatment including corrective surgery, spectacles, hearing aids, and nutritional supplements is completely free through District Early Intervention Centres (DEICs). No income criteria, no documents needed for screening.
